description_long: This sculpture depicts two life-sized mountain goats or ibexes engaged in a dramatic head-butting contest, their horns locked in a dynamic display of strength and rivalry. The animals are rendered in a realistic style, with detailed musculature and textured fur, standing on a stylized rocky base that mimics a natural mountainous terrain. The base is slightly elevated and angular, giving the impression of a rugged outdoor setting. The goats' postures are tense and energetic, with one rearing up and the other bracing itself, emphasizing the intensity of their interaction. The surface of the sculpture appears to be made of bronze or a similar metal, with a weathered patina that adds depth and character. The entire piece is set on a patch of ground with some grass visible around the edges, suggesting an outdoor installation.
description: A realistic bronze sculpture of two mountain goats locked in a head-butting contest atop a rugged, rocky base, with detailed musculature and textured fur.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing both goats facing each other, horns interlocked, and the full rocky base beneath them.
description_view_1: Side angle emphasizing the length of the sculpture and the dynamic posture of the goats, with one goat's head and horns more prominent.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the backs of both goats and the angular shape of the base, with the animals' tails and hind legs visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side angle, highlighting the other goat's head and the layered rocky base, with a clear view of the animals' bodies from behind.
attribute_subject_matter: two mountain goats in a head-butting contest
attribute_location_context: The sculpture is installed outdoors on a patch of grass, likely in a park or public plaza.
